description: CE3.0 sibling of evidence-submission-decision-v1, for Visa fraud reason 10.4 disputes Stripe flags CE3.0-eligible (enhanced_eligibility_types contains visa_compelling_evidence_3). Compelling Evidence 3.0 is a STRUCTURED signed assertion: a disputed transaction plus >=2 prior undisputed transactions whose customer signals (purchase IP / device / shipping / email / account) match, qualifying the merchant for a liability shift to the issuer. The recipe stages the exact visa_compelling_evidence_3 object (submit=false), reads the SoR-rendered qualification (evidence_details.enhanced_eligibility.visa_compelling_evidence_3.status: requires_action -> qualified | not_qualified), and a human principal authorizes a QUALIFIED package BEFORE the irreversible submit=true. Two facts are notarized the flat evidence type can't express: (1) ce3EligibilityStatus - the qualification is rendered by the SoR (Stripe, proxying Visa), is a STRUCTURAL gate INDEPENDENT of the won/lost outcome, and is held as the network's word, not adjudicated by us; (2) evidencePackageHash binds the EXACT visa_compelling_evidence_3 bytes submitted - so 'what did the merchant assert, and did they alter it after sign-off?' is cryptographically answerable. The engine enforces structural completeness at validation: >=2 priors each with charge + product_description, and the disputed transaction must carry product_description + merchandiseOrServices plus a valid matching combination (two main, or one main + one secondary); Stripe enforces the cross-transaction qualification. GATE + SoD ARE ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see README): gateMode:auto can override defaultGateMode:principal (F-791) and principal==performer self-commitment is not rejected (F-758). The orchestrator MUST route through the principal gate, MUST NOT set gateMode:auto, MUST provision distinct performer+principal identities, and SHOULD only propose a package whose staged status == qualified.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...) this CE3.0 decision authorizes a response to.

disputeRef.description: recordId of the chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 this decision descends from (chain lineage).

reason.description: CE3.0 applies only to Visa fraud reason 10.4 — Stripe reports this as reason=fraudulent. Pinned: a non-fraud dispute is not CE3.0-eligible.

network.description: CE3.0 is a Visa program. Pinned to visa.

evidenceDueBy.description: Carried from the filing so a decision rendered after the deadline is observable on-chain.

ce3EligibilityStatus.description: The SoR-rendered CE3.0 qualification for the EXACT staged payload, read from Stripe's evidence_details.enhanced_eligibility.visa_compelling_evidence_3.status after staging (submit=false). This is the NETWORK's structural verdict on the package, INDEPENDENT of the eventual won/lost outcome — AGLedger holds it, does not compute it. Recipe convention: only propose/authorize when == qualified.

evidencePackageHash.description: sha256:<hex> of the exact, canonicalized visa_compelling_evidence_3 object the recipe will submit. Binds the authorization to specific structured bytes.

ce3EligibilityStatus.description: Must equal the record's criteria.ce3EligibilityStatus — the SoR qualification the principal is authorizing.

evidencePackageHash.description: Must equal the record's criteria.evidencePackageHash — sha256 of the exact visa_compelling_evidence_3 object submitted to Stripe.

disputedTransaction.description: Stripe evidence.enhanced_evidence.visa_compelling_evidence_3.disputed_transaction. Must carry product_description + merchandise_or_services AND satisfy Visa's evidence-matching rule (two main elements, or one main + one secondary). Main = customer_purchase_ip, customer_device_fingerprint, customer_device_id (fingerprint+id cannot be paired). Secondary = shipping_address, customer_email_address, customer_account_id.

priorUndisputedTransactions.description: Stripe evidence.enhanced_evidence.visa_compelling_evidence_3.prior_undisputed_transactions — >=2 prior undisputed charges whose signals match the disputed transaction. Each must carry a charge id + product_description.

uncategorizedText.description: Stripe evidence.uncategorized_text — optional free narrative submitted alongside. In test mode the strings winning_evidence / losing_evidence force the outcome.
